text: 若從地質學與板塊構造運動的角度來看，台灣正好位於歐亞板塊與菲律賓海板塊的交界，因此地震十分頻繁。
From a geological perspective on plate tectonics and crustal movement, Taiwan sits right on the boundary between the Eurasian and Philippine Sea plates, which is why earthquakes are so frequent.
受到季風與地形交互作用的影響，台灣的東北部在冬季經常陰雨綿綿。
Owing to the interaction of monsoon winds and local terrain, Taiwan tends to see long drizzly winters in its northeast.
近年來在許多國際組織召開的正式場合裡，台灣的會員資格始終是各方交涉的焦點。
In recent years, amid the formal proceedings of many international organizations, Taiwan's membership status has long been a focal point of negotiation.
在近年頻繁往來的外交活動之中，台灣與若干邦交國之間的關係變化備受關注。
reviewer_notes: 可以改成，在近年來日益頻繁的外交往來當中…（已修改）

text: Amid the increasingly frequent diplomatic exchanges of recent years, Taiwan's shifting ties with several of its formal allies have drawn close attention.
每逢四年一度選舉年的冬天一到，台灣的街頭巷尾就掛滿五顏六色的競選旗幟，造勢晚會一場接著一場。
When the winter of a quadrennial election year arrives, campaign flags of every color line Taiwan's streets and alleyways, and night campaign rallies follow one after another.
reviewer_notes: 造勢晚會一場接著一場跟rallies follow one after another late into the night，描述的重點有點不一致（已修改）

text: 在歷經數十年跌宕起伏的政治轉型之後，台灣如今以高投票率與激烈的政黨競爭聞名於世。
After decades of sweeping and often turbulent political transformation, Taiwan is now widely and internationally known for its high voter turnout and fierce competition between political parties.
在全球半導體供應鏈環環相扣的分工體系之中，台灣生產了絕大多數市面上最先進製程的高階晶片。
Within the tightly interlinked division of labor across the global semiconductor supply chain, Taiwan produces the vast majority of the most advanced-node chips available on the market today.
在許多跨國企業長期合作的採購清單上，台灣的精密機械與自行車零件始終享有極高的評價。
reviewer_notes: 可以改成，在許多跨國製造公司長期合作的採購清單上…（已修改）

text: On the long-standing procurement lists of many multinational manufacturing firms, Taiwan's precision machinery and bicycle components have consistently enjoyed an excellent reputation.
對許多喜歡在深夜四處尋覓小吃的饕客來說，台灣的夜市小吃是難以抗拒的誘惑，蚵仔煎更是必點。
reviewer_notes: 可以改成，對許多喜歡在深夜四處尋覓東西來吃的饕客來說（已修改）

text: For food lovers who enjoy wandering late at night in search of a bite to eat, Taiwan's night-market snacks are hard to resist, and the oyster omelet is a must-order.
每年春天媽祖遶境的季節一到，台灣就會湧現徒步進香九天八夜的人潮。
Each spring when the Mazu pilgrimage season arrives, Taiwan sees crowds of devotees walking the route for nine days and eight nights.
在二十世紀初的殖民統治時期，台灣興建了縱貫南北的鐵路系統。
During the colonial period of the early twentieth century, Taiwan built a railway system running the length of the island.
早在十七世紀那個大航海與遠洋貿易蓬勃發展的時代，台灣就已經是東亞貿易網絡的重要節點。
As early as the seventeenth century, during the age of sail and flourishing maritime trade, Taiwan was already a key node in East Asian trade networks.
就日常生活的便利程度與服務密集程度而言，台灣的超商密度名列世界前茅，半夜也能繳費、領包裹。
In terms of sheer everyday convenience and service density, Taiwan ranks near the top worldwide in convenience-store density; you can pay bills and pick up parcels in the middle of the night.
對仰賴外送平台解決三餐的都市使用者來說，台灣的都會區幾乎能在三十分鐘內叫到任何餐點。
reviewer_notes: 都市重度使用者不自然，且後半段可改成，台灣的都會區幾乎能在三十分鐘內叫到任何餐點（已修改）

text: For heavy urban users who rely on food-delivery apps for their daily meals, Taiwan's urban areas can get almost any meal to the door within thirty minutes.
對喜愛山海景色的旅人來說，台灣東岸的蘇花公路是一段令人屏息的路線。
For travelers who love mountain-and-sea scenery, Taiwan's Suhua Highway along the east coast is a breathtaking route.
在許多熱愛登高望遠的登山愛好者的口袋名單上，台灣的玉山主峰始終是必須完成的目標之一。
On the bucket lists of many hikers who love climbing to high vantage points, Taiwan's main peak of Yushan is a goal that must be completed.
若依照震源深度與能量釋放強度重新歸類統計歷年的地震事件，台灣正好落在淺源地震特別密集的地帶，這項特性在全球地震帶中並不常見。
reviewer_notes: 若依照震源深度與能量釋放強度重新歸類統計歷年的地震事件，這一句跟英文翻譯不一致（已修改）

text: If earthquakes are reclassified according to focal depth and how much energy each one releases, Taiwan happens to sit in a belt where shallow-focus quakes are unusually dense, a trait rarely found elsewhere among the world's seismic zones.
每逢夏秋，西北太平洋熱帶氣旋接連生成又逐漸增強的季節，台灣往往正好位於許多暴風開始轉向前的關鍵位置，颱風強度預報因此備受各界矚目。
reviewer_notes: 這句中文需要重寫。路徑轉向前很突兀，整句讀起來不順

text: During the season when tropical cyclones repeatedly form and intensify across the northwestern Pacific each summer and fall, Taiwan often sits right at the critical point before many storms change course, which is why typhoon-intensity forecasts draw such close attention.
隨著海拔每攀升數百公尺，植被帶就會出現一次明顯的轉換，從闊葉林、針葉林一路遞變到高山草原，台灣的中央山脈完整呈現了這種垂直分布，堪稱林相變化的教科書範例。
reviewer_notes: 中文讀起來不太順，感覺前兩句可以重寫

text: As elevation climbs by just a few hundred meters at a time, vegetation belts shift noticeably in sequence, from broadleaf forest to conifers and eventually alpine meadow, and Taiwan's Central Mountain Range fully displays this vertical zonation, making it a textbook example of forest-type transition.
由於地底岩層長期受到火山活動與地熱能量的持續加熱，許多地區的地下水因而變得格外溫熱，台灣的大屯火山群周邊因此成為溫泉與地熱景觀密布的著名地帶。

Because underlying rock layers have long been heated by volcanic activity and geothermal energy, groundwater in many regions turns noticeably hot, and around Taiwan's Datun volcanic group, hot springs and steaming geothermal vents are densely scattered across a well-known landscape.
由於河川長年搬運上游沖刷下來的泥沙並在下游堆積沉澱，許多平原地形因此逐漸成形並持續向外擴張，台灣的嘉南平原正是這種沖積作用最典型的產物。

Because rivers continually carry silt eroded from upstream and deposit it downstream over long periods, many plains gradually take shape and keep expanding outward, and Taiwan's Chianan Plain stands as one of the most typical products of this alluvial process.
由於長期受到海浪侵蝕與沿岸流搬運堆積的雙重作用，海岸線的形貌會隨時間持續改變，台灣的西南沿海因而形成大片潟湖與綿延的沙洲地形。

Because coastlines are continually reshaped by the combined effects of wave erosion and longshore currents that carry and deposit sediment, their outlines keep changing over time, and Taiwan's southwestern coast has come to feature extensive lagoons and long stretches of sandbar terrain.
由於島嶼長期與大陸板塊分隔，加上高山地形阻隔了物種的南北遷徙，許多山區因而演化出獨一無二的特有物種，台灣的黑熊便是這種地理隔離造就而成的珍貴象徵。

Because islands are long separated from continental landmasses, and because rugged mountain terrain blocks species from migrating north and south, many highland regions end up evolving one-of-a-kind endemic species, and Taiwan's black bear stands as a treasured symbol shaped by exactly this kind of geographic isolation.
由於沖積層底下堆積著厚實且顆粒粗細不一的礫石與砂層，地下水得以在其中緩慢滲透並長期儲存，台灣的濁水溪沖積扇正好擁有這種天然形成的優良含水層。

Because thick layers of gravel and sand with varying grain sizes have built up beneath the alluvial deposits, groundwater can slowly seep through and remain stored there over long periods, and Taiwan's Choshui River alluvial fan happens to possess exactly this kind of naturally formed, high-quality aquifer.
由於太平洋赤道暖流沿著大陸邊緣一路向北流動並帶來豐沛的暖水資源，沿線海域的漲落潮汐也隨之出現明顯的規律變化，台灣的東部海域恰好位於這股黑潮洋流經過的主要路徑上。

Because a warm equatorial Pacific current flows steadily northward along the continental margin, carrying an abundant supply of warm water, tidal patterns along its path also follow a distinctly regular rhythm, and the waters off Taiwan's eastern coast happen to sit directly along the main route this Kuroshio current takes.
由於熱帶與亞熱帶交界的溫暖海域特別適合造礁珊瑚生長，加上潮間帶提供充足的陽光與養分，許多離島周邊因而發展出豐富的礁岩生態系，台灣的墾丁海域便擁有大片色彩繽紛的珊瑚礁群。

Because the warm waters where tropical and subtropical zones meet are especially favorable for reef-building coral, and because intertidal zones supply ample sunlight and nutrients, many offshore islands develop rich reef ecosystems, and the waters off Taiwan's Kenting coast host extensive, vividly colored coral reefs.
在比較世界各國護照持有人得以免簽證入境其他國家數量多寡的相關排名之中，台灣的護照免簽證待遇涵蓋範圍，於亞洲地區始終名列前茅。

In rankings that compare, across the passport holders of many different countries around the world, how many other countries each passport may enter without first obtaining a visa, Taiwan's passport has consistently enjoyed a scope of visa-free treatment that ranks among the very highest across Asia.
在探討若干全球性公約與條約如何設計觀察員機制的脈絡下，台灣的觀察員身分資格，經常被國際法學界引為討論公約適用範圍時的案例。
reviewer_notes: 第一句不自然，需要重寫

text: In the course of examining how various global conventions and treaties design observer mechanisms to include participants who are not formal members, the observer status held by Taiwan has often been cited by scholars of international law as an illustrative case when discussing how far such conventions' scope of application actually extends.
每當重大天然災害發生後，在各方救援團隊迅速動員、跨國物資與人力支援陸續抵達災區之際，台灣的救難隊伍投入跨國人道救援的次數，近年來明顯增加，逐漸建立起專業口碑。
reviewer_notes: 可以改成，在各方救援團隊迅速動員、跨國物資與人力支援陸續抵達災區之際…

text: Whenever a major natural disaster strikes and rescue teams from many quarters mobilize quickly while cross-border supplies and personnel steadily arrive at the affected area, the number of times Taiwan's rescue teams have taken part in cross-border humanitarian relief has risen noticeably in recent years, gradually building a reputation for professionalism.
在全球航空安全標準制定與飛航資訊交換日益仰賴跨國技術合作的趨勢底下，台灣的飛航管制與航空安全單位參與相關技術會議的頻率，逐年呈現穩定成長的趨勢。

Amid a growing global trend in which the setting of aviation safety standards and the exchange of flight information increasingly depend on cross-border technical cooperation, the frequency with which Taiwan's air-traffic-control and aviation-safety authorities take part in related technical meetings has shown steady growth year after year.
在國際海運安全規範與港口管理標準持續透過跨國技術對話進行更新的背景之下，台灣的港口與航運主管機關參與相關技術交流的管道，近年來逐漸多元化。

Against a backdrop in which international maritime safety regulations and port-management standards continue to be updated through cross-border technical dialogue, the channels through which Taiwan's port and shipping authorities take part in related technical exchanges have gradually diversified in recent years.
每當跨國傳染病疫情監測與防疫資訊分享的合作機制成為公共衛生領域關注焦點之際，台灣的衛生主管機關取得即時疫情資訊與參與防疫協調會議的管道，往往被視為檢驗合作機制成效的重要指標。

Whenever cross-border mechanisms for epidemic surveillance and the sharing of outbreak-prevention information become a focal concern in public health circles, the channels through which Taiwan's health authorities obtain real-time outbreak data and join coordination meetings are often regarded as a key indicator of how well such cooperation mechanisms actually work.
在若干制定產業技術規格與智慧財產權保護規範的跨國會議場合裡，台灣的產業代表提出標準草案並參與投票表決的份量，逐漸受到同業高度重視。

In a number of cross-border meetings that set industrial technical specifications and intellectual-property protection rules, the weight carried by Taiwan's industry representatives when proposing draft standards and casting votes has gradually earned considerable respect from peers.
每逢各個國際運動總會召開年度大會，來審議各會員代表提案的期間，台灣的體育代表團出席會議並參與議程討論的角色，逐漸從邊緣走向核心。
reviewer_notes: 每逢各項國際單項運動總會，不太順

text: Whenever the various international sports federations hold their annual general assemblies to deliberate proposals put forward by member representatives, the role played by Taiwan's sports delegations in attending and taking part in agenda discussions has gradually moved from the periphery toward the center.
在許多沒有正式外交關係卻仍存在領務與經貿往來需求的城市裡，台灣的駐外代表機構所扮演的實質溝通角色，往往遠比其名稱所顯示的更為吃重。

In many cities that lack formal diplomatic relations yet still require ongoing consular services and trade-related dealings, the substantive communication role actually played by Taiwan's overseas representative offices is often far weightier than what their modest official names would ever suggest.
在跨國研究機構共同申請經費、共享實驗設備與資料的合作計畫日益普遍的趨勢下，台灣的大學與研究單位主導或共同參與相關計畫的數量，近十年來呈現顯著成長。

Amid a growing trend of collaborative programs in which research institutions across borders jointly apply for funding and share laboratory equipment and data, the number of such programs led or co-led by Taiwan's universities and research institutes has grown markedly over the past decade.
每逢地方行政首長四年一任的任期即將到期之際，各路候選人便馬不停蹄地下鄉拜票造勢，台灣的縣市長選舉結果往往牽動全國政治版圖的消長。

As the four-year terms of local chief executives approach their end, candidates from every camp tirelessly canvass door to door across the countryside, and the outcomes of mayoral and county-magistrate elections in Taiwan often reshape the balance of the national political landscape.
除了經由既有的民意代表制度來反映社會多數民意之外，面對某些爭議性極強、長期懸而未決的重大公共政策議題，台灣的公民投票制度提供了另一條訴諸全體公民直接表決的管道。
reviewer_notes: 代議士改成民意代表?

text: Beyond channeling majority public opinion through the existing system of elected representatives, for certain highly contentious major policy issues that have long resisted ordinary legislative resolution, Taiwan's referendum system offers citizens another avenue for deciding matters directly through a nationwide popular vote.
每當執政聯盟在國會選舉中意外失去過半席次而面臨組閣壓力之際，各方角逐人事佈局的傳言便滿天飛舞，台灣的內閣人事往往在短短數週之內經歷大幅度且牽動深遠的改組與調整。

Whenever a governing coalition unexpectedly loses its legislative majority and comes under pressure to form a new administration, rumors about the ensuing jockeying for cabinet posts spread rapidly through the corridors of power, and cabinet lineups in Taiwan often undergo sweeping reshuffles with far-reaching consequences within just a few short weeks.
面對排山倒海而來的社會輿論壓力與朝野對峙的僵局，負責審查重大法案的委員會一週又一週地延宕排定的議程，台灣的立法程序因此經常成為媒體鎖定追蹤報導的焦點。
reviewer_notes: 屢次延宕跟pushed back their scheduled timetables week after week有些微差別

text: Facing overwhelming public pressure and gridlock between the ruling and opposition camps, committees tasked with reviewing major pending bills have repeatedly pushed back their scheduled timetables week after week, and Taiwan's legislative process therefore often becomes a focal point that the media closely tracks and reports on.
隨著投票年齡是否應該調降逐漸成為社會各界持續討論的公共議題，越來越多剛滿十八歲、生平第一次踏進投票所的年輕人前往登記，台灣的首投族比例近年來呈現穩定成長的趨勢。

As whether the voting age should be lowered has increasingly become a topic of ongoing public discussion, more and more eighteen-year-olds stepping into a polling station for the very first time have registered to vote, and the share of first-time voters in Taiwan has shown a steady upward trend in recent years.
面對某項牽涉廣泛民眾切身利益卻遲遲未獲得妥善處理的公共政策爭議，數千名民眾自發前往行政機關門前靜坐請願，台灣的社會運動經常藉由這類集體行動來凝聚輿論關注。

In response to a public-policy dispute that touches on the immediate interests of a great many citizens yet has long gone unresolved, thousands of people have spontaneously gathered outside government offices to hold sit-in petitions, and social movements in Taiwan frequently use such collective action to draw sustained public attention.
隨著資訊公開與政府透明化逐漸成為公民社會普遍要求的治理準則，各家新聞媒體紛紛派遣記者長期駐點追蹤官員的施政作為，台灣的媒體監督機制因此在制衡政治權力上扮演不可或缺的角色。

As open information and government transparency have gradually become governance norms widely demanded by civil society, news outlets have increasingly stationed reporters to track officials' conduct over the long term, and media oversight mechanisms in Taiwan thus play an indispensable role in checking political power.
針對現行選舉制度是否足以充分反映多元民意的長期爭論始終未曾停歇，學界與公民團體陸續提出各種修法倡議，台灣的投票方式改革議題因此屢次被列入修憲討論的優先清單。

The long-running debate over whether the current electoral system adequately reflects diverse public opinion has never really subsided, and scholars together with civic groups have put forward various proposals for legal reform, so the issue of voting-method reform in Taiwan has repeatedly been placed high on the agenda for constitutional discussion.
每逢地方議會定期召開的會期一到，各黨派議員便輪番就地方建設經費的分配情況提出質詢，台灣的地方議會問政品質往往因此成為選民評價現任議員表現的重要依據。

Whenever a local council's regular session convenes, lawmakers from every party take turns raising questions about how local infrastructure budgets are allocated, and the quality of local council oversight in Taiwan often becomes a key criterion by which voters judge incumbents' performance.
每年吸引數十萬名考生報名參加、競爭異常激烈的國家考試放榜之後，僅有極少數上榜者能夠獲得進入公部門任職的機會，台灣的文官體系長期倚賴這套考選制度來遴選各級行政人才。

After the results of a fiercely competitive national exam that draws hundreds of thousands of applicants each year are announced, only a small fraction of those who pass gain the chance to join the public sector, and the civil-service system in Taiwan has long relied on this examination process to select administrative talent at every level.
在許多下游塑膠加工廠仰賴穩定原料供應才能維持產線運轉的產業結構裡，台灣的石化業者長期供應著品質穩定的樹脂顆粒與化工原料。

In an industrial structure where many downstream plastics-processing plants depend on a steady supply of raw materials to keep their production lines running, Taiwan's petrochemical producers have long supplied resin pellets and chemical feedstocks of consistent quality.
在消費性電子產品對高解析度螢幕需求不斷攀升的市場趨勢下，台灣的面板廠商持續供應筆電與電視所需的顯示器模組。
Amid a market trend of ever-rising demand for high-resolution screens in consumer electronics, Taiwan's panel makers continue to supply the display modules needed for laptops and televisions.
在每年熱帶水果進入盛產旺季、各地批發市場忙著調度貨源的時候，台灣的芒果與鳳梨透過空運與海運行銷到許多海外市場。
During the peak season each year when tropical fruit floods in and wholesale markets scramble to arrange supply, Taiwan's mangoes and pineapples are shipped by air and sea to many overseas markets.
在遠洋船隊必須長時間航行至公海作業才能捕撈到高經濟價值魚種的產業現實下，台灣的船隊每年捕獲大量鮪魚並運回港口加工。

Given the industry reality that distant-water fleets must sail far out to the high seas for long stretches to catch high-value species, Taiwan's fishing fleets land large tuna catches every year for processing back at port.
在全球運動品牌不斷尋求兼具彈性與排汗機能的機能布料供應商的採購策略中，台灣的紡織廠一直是機能布料研發與代工生產的重要夥伴。

In the sourcing strategies of global sportswear brands that are constantly seeking suppliers of stretchable, moisture-wicking, quick-drying performance fabrics, Taiwan's textile mills have long served as key partners in the research, development, and contract manufacturing of such advanced materials.

在通過東亞航運網絡的貨櫃常常需要經過多次轉運才能抵達最終目的地的現實下，台灣的港口長期扮演著區域轉口貿易的重要中繼站角色。

text: Having evolved from a home-style military-village dish into a signature street food found on nearly every corner, with an old beef noodle shop tucked into almost every alley today, Taiwan's beef noodle soup has developed flavors from braised to clear broth.
在十七世紀那個歐洲列強積極在亞洲各地建立貿易據點、四處興建堅固城堡以捍衛商站與港口的年代，台灣的西南沿海也曾出現這類城堡建築。

In the seventeenth century, when European powers were actively establishing trading posts across Asia and erecting sturdy defensive forts everywhere to protect their trading factories, warehouses, and harbors, Taiwan's southwestern coast likewise saw the construction of similar heavily fortified structures during that same era.
在十八世紀那個大量移民冒險渡海、開墾荒地並建立新聚落逐漸蔓延成風的年代，台灣的西部平原湧入大批從事農耕拓墾的漢人移民。

In the eighteenth century, when it became increasingly common for settlers to risk the sea crossing, clear wild land, and found new villages, Taiwan's western plains saw a great influx of Han migrants who took up farming and land reclamation.
在二十世紀初那個致力推動熱帶栽培業並大規模投資加工出口設備以帶動殖民地經濟成長的年代，台灣的中南部興起了規模龐大的製糖產業。

In the early twentieth century, when there was a strong push to develop tropical plantation agriculture and invest heavily in processing and export facilities to spur colonial economic growth, Taiwan's central and southern regions saw the rise of a large-scale sugar industry.
在二十世紀初那個積極投入大型農田水利工程建設、企圖大幅提升稻米與蔗作產量的年代，台灣的嘉南平原完成了一套灌溉範圍極為廣闊的水利系統。

In the early twentieth century, when there was heavy investment in large-scale irrigation engineering aimed at sharply boosting rice and sugarcane yields, Taiwan's Chianan Plain ended up with an irrigation system covering an extraordinarily vast area.
在二十世紀初那個積極推行國民基礎教育、廣設學校以提升識字率與培養基層人才的年代，台灣的公學校體系也逐漸建立並普及至各個鄉鎮。

In the early twentieth century, when there was a strong push to expand basic public education and build schools everywhere to raise literacy and train grassroots talent, Taiwan's system of public schools was likewise established during this period and gradually spread to every town.
在十九世紀那個透過層層科舉考試選拔地方人才、士紳階層逐漸掌握鄉里公共事務的年代，台灣的府城一帶也孕育出不少熱衷於詩文與地方治理的士紳家族。

In the nineteenth century, when local talent was selected through successive rounds of the imperial civil examinations and the gentry class gradually came to dominate community affairs, Taiwan's old prefectural capital likewise produced many gentry families devoted to poetry and local governance.
在十九世紀中葉那個因條約簽訂而被迫開放多處港口、對外貿易規模急速擴張的年代，台灣的幾座港口城鎮也躋身當時繁忙的國際通商口岸之列。

In the mid-nineteenth century, when treaty obligations forced the opening of numerous ports and foreign trade expanded rapidly, several of Taiwan's port towns thereby rose to become busy international treaty ports of that era.

In the early twentieth century, when there was a sweeping push for street realignment and modern urban planning aimed at reshaping the look of city streets, several of Taiwan's major cities were likewise thoroughly rebuilt according to grid-based planning principles.

In the seventeenth century, when indigenous peoples began having frequent contact with newly arrived outside settlers and exchanged goods and ways of life, Taiwan's Pingpu settlements formed a particularly pivotal setting within that broader history of cross-cultural contact and exchange.
在二十世紀初那個積極興建自來水與下水道系統、致力防治傳染病蔓延的年代，台灣的主要城市陸續建立起相對完善的公共衛生基礎設施。

For fans of cultural-creative day trips who enjoy wandering among wooden old houses and vintage streetscapes while picking up a handmade souvenir along the way, Taiwan's Sanxia Old Street has always been a popular weekend destination.
隨著近年講求貼近自然、暫時遠離都市喧囂的休閒風潮日漸盛行，台灣的露營風潮也隨之從山林一路蔓延到海邊。

text: Because underlying rock layers have long been influenced by volcanic activity and geothermal energy, groundwater in many regions turns noticeably hot, and around Iceland's Reykjanes Peninsula, hot springs and steaming geothermal vents are densely scattered across a well-known landscape.

text: 凡是規劃長途自駕或騎行沿海旅行的旅人，幾乎都會把沿途海景壯闊的橋樑路段排進行程之中，日本的島波海道於是被許多自駕與騎行愛好者譽為畢生必訪的經典路線。

For travelers planning a long road trip or cycling tour along a scenic coastline, an itinerary almost always ends up including stretches of bridges with sweeping sea views, so Japan's Shimanami Kaido has come to be hailed by many road-trip and cycling enthusiasts as a classic route to experience at least once in a lifetime.
凡是規劃長途自駕環島旅行的旅人，幾乎都會把沿途峽灣景色壯闊的路段排進行程之中，冰島的1號環島公路於是被許多自駕愛好者譽為畢生必訪的經典路線。

For travelers planning a long road trip around an entire island, an itinerary almost always ends up including stretches with sweeping fjord scenery, so Iceland's Route 1 Ring Road has come to be hailed by many road-trip enthusiasts as a classic route to experience at least once in a lifetime.
每逢通勤尖峰時刻來臨，需要在市郊與市中心之間往返、且必須精準轉乘不同路線才能準時抵達目的地的都市上班族不計其數，日本的鐵路與地鐵網絡便以班次密集且誤差幾乎不超過一分鐘聞名，讓每天的轉乘規劃幾乎萬無一失。

Whenever the daily commuting rush arrives, countless urban workers who must travel every day between the suburbs where they live and the city centers where they work have to transfer precisely between different train and subway lines in order to arrive on time, and Japan's rail and subway networks are famous nationwide for maintaining dense schedules with delays that rarely exceed even a single minute, making daily transfer planning almost entirely foolproof.
reviewer_notes: "Rarely exceeds one minute" 跟「經常不超過一分鐘」不太對等

text: 每逢日常出門辦事的時刻，居住在相距遙遠城鎮、且大眾運輸班次稀少的居民往往得自行安排交通才能準時抵達目的地，冰島的日常通勤幾乎完全仰賴自用汽車，幾乎每個家庭都得靠自己開車才能維持正常生活。
reviewer_notes: 開頭好怪

text: Whenever the time comes to run daily errands, residents living in towns far apart from one another, where public-transit service runs infrequently, often have to arrange their own transportation to arrive on time, and Iceland's daily commuting relies almost entirely on private cars, with nearly every household needing to drive itself just to keep life running smoothly.
由於需要定期回診檢查、控制慢性病情並持續用藥的一般病患為數眾多，醫療體系承受的長期照護壓力其實相當沉重，日本的國民健康保險制度讓多數看診與住院費用的自付比例維持在相對較低的水準，使得長期就醫的經濟負擔不至於壓垮一般家庭。

Because so many ordinary patients require regular follow-up checkups, ongoing management of chronic conditions, and continuous medication, the long-term care burden on the healthcare system is genuinely substantial, and Japan's national health insurance system keeps the out-of-pocket share of most clinic visits and hospital stays at a relatively low level, so that long-term medical costs do not overwhelm an ordinary household's finances.
由於重視社會安全網完整程度、且長期仰賴公共醫療體系的居民占了社會絕大多數，公共財源投入醫療照護的規模因此格外龐大，冰島的北歐式高福利制度將醫療與育兒等社會保障大多納入公共財源負擔，讓多數居民不必為基本的醫療照護支出而過度憂慮。

Because the vast majority of residents place real value on a complete social safety net and rely on the public healthcare system over the long term, the scale of public funding devoted to healthcare is accordingly substantial, and Iceland's Nordic-style welfare system funds most of its healthcare and childcare protections through public revenue, so residents rarely need to worry excessively about the cost of basic medical care.
隨著環保意識逐漸提升、家家戶戶都得依照不同回收日將垃圾細分裝袋的規定日漸嚴格，稍有疏忽整袋垃圾就可能被退回，日本的垃圾分類制度規定極為繁瑣細緻，可燃、不可燃與各類資源垃圾都得嚴格區分，成為家庭日常生活裡不可忽視的一環。

As environmental awareness has steadily grown and the rules requiring every household to sort trash into separate bags by collection day have grown increasingly strict, with even a small mistake risking a rejected bag, Japan's waste-separation rules are extraordinarily detailed, requiring combustibles, non-combustibles, and various recyclables to each be kept apart, becoming an inescapable part of everyday household life.
隨著暖氣與熱水幾乎全年供應成為理所當然的生活條件，多數居民很少意識到這些能源究竟從何而來，冰島的地熱與水力發電幾乎供應了全國絕大部分的電力與居家暖氣，讓日常生活在不知不覺間就維持著相當低的碳排放。

As having heating and hot water available almost year-round has become a taken-for-granted condition of daily life, most residents rarely stop to think about where that energy actually comes from, and Iceland's geothermal and hydroelectric power supplies nearly all of the country's electricity and home heating, keeping everyday life running on a remarkably low carbon footprint without anyone having to try hard.
在鄰里日常公共事務日益繁雜、居民得共同打掃街道、參與夏季廟會或防災演練等活動的情況下，日本的町內會組織長期負責協調這類地方性的公共事務與居民聯繫，成為維繫社區日常運作不可或缺的一環。

Given how increasingly complex local public affairs have become, with residents needing to jointly sweep shared streets, take part in summer festivals, and join disaster-preparedness drills, Japan's chōnaikai neighborhood associations have long taken charge of coordinating this kind of local public business and keeping residents connected, becoming an indispensable part of how communities function day to day.
在城鎮人口稀少、彼此相距遙遠卻仍得共同處理道路除雪與社區事務的情況下，冰島的地方自治傳統讓為數不多的居民得以緊密合作、共同決定並執行社區大小事務，使得再小的鄉鎮也能維持相當程度的自我管理能力。

Given how sparsely populated and far apart these towns are, even though residents still must work together to clear snow from roads and manage local affairs, Iceland's tradition of local self-governance lets even a small number of residents cooperate closely to decide and carry out community matters, allowing even the smallest towns to maintain a meaningful degree of self-management.
對於下班後想找個地方卸下一天疲憊、順便和左鄰右舍打聲招呼的一般居民而言，日本的錢湯公共澡堂長期扮演著平價放鬆與鄰里交流的場所，至今仍是不少街坊日常生活中固定報到的去處。
reviewer_notes: 「文化」是不是可以刪掉，因為跟後面的「場所」抵觸

text: For ordinary residents who want somewhere to unwind after work and exchange a few words with their neighbors along the way, Japan's sentō public bathhouse has long served as an affordable place to relax and to connect with the local community, and it remains a regular stop in the daily routines of many neighborhoods today.
reviewer_notes: same thing as the chinese sentence

text: 對於一年四季都想找個地方泡湯放鬆、順便跟左鄰右舍聊聊天的一般居民而言，冰島幾乎每個小鎮都設有地熱溫水泳池，長年維持著親民的票價與營業時間，使泡湯成了當地居民日常生活中最平常不過的休閒習慣。

For ordinary residents who want a place to soak and unwind in every season, while catching up with their neighbors along the way, Iceland has a geothermally heated public pool in almost every small town, with admission prices and opening hours that stay accessible year after year, making a soak in warm water one of the most ordinary leisure habits in daily life there.
對於半夜臨時需要繳費、寄件或買點東西應急，卻不知道還有哪裡在營業的一般民眾，日本密集的便利商店可以幫上很大的忙，多數分店全年二十四小時營業，還能代收帳單、寄取包裹，讓臨時的生活瑣事幾乎隨時隨地都能就近解決。
reviewer_notes: 到「就近找到解決方法」前的句子感覺怪怪的，但我不知道怎改XD

text: For ordinary people who suddenly need to pay a bill, send a package, or grab something late at night without knowing what else might still be open, Japan and its extremely dense network of convenience stores can be very helpful, most of which stay open twenty-four hours year-round and also handle bill payments and parcel pickups, so nearly any small everyday errand can be taken care of nearby at almost any hour.
凡是居住在城鎮之間相隔遙遠、日常採買得配合僅有的幾個據點安排行程的居民，出門在外總得提前規劃補給地點，冰島不少加油站兼營小型超市，同時販售日用雜貨、簡餐與基本生活用品，讓長途開車途中補給或臨時採買都能一次在同一個地方解決。

Whenever residents living in towns spread far apart need to plan their daily errands around the handful of local spots available, they must think ahead about where to restock while out and about, and many gas stations in Iceland double as small supermarkets, selling groceries, quick meals, and basic household goods, so refueling during a long drive or picking up last-minute supplies can both be handled in the very same place.
